mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing cycle that lowers discount rates, boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Excessively low inflation (CPIAUCSL < 220) signals weak demand, prompting central banks to maintain accommodative policy and drive SPX positive returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative when CPIAUCSL remains below 220 for 3+ months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and employment (PAYEMS) signals robust economic activity, driving corporate earnings and positive SPX returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative when both INDPRO and PAYEMS rise by >1% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening labor market (UNRATE < 6%) drives wage inflation, prompting monetary policy tightening and negative SPX returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive when UNRATE remains below 6% for 3+ months.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST > 850k) indicate resilient demand, boosting construction-related sectors and generating positive SPX returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative when HOUST exceeds 850k for 2+ months.

mechanism_narrative: Declining consumer spending (RSAFS < 400k) signals weak demand, leading to lower corporate revenues and negative SPX returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive when RSAFS remains below 400k for 3+ months.

mechanism_narrative: Weak industrial production (INDPRO < 95) combined with high unemployment (UNRATE > 7%) signals economic stagnation, driving SPX negative returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive when INDPRO < 95 and UNRATE > 7% simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS < 100k) signals economic contraction, prompting risk-off sentiment and negative SPX returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive when PAYEMS falls below 100k for 2+ months.
